results.title: Recent AI Product Launches
results.content: half vs half-duplex Doubao. - Willow.ai launches Atlas-1, a new frontier STT model built on human-powered transcription infrastructure that claims to beat ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI. - Telnyx launches LiveKit on Telnyx, a hosted platform running LiveKit agents on Telnyx infrastructure with 50% lower cost and sub-200ms latency. - Natter raises $23M Series A led by Renegade Partners to replace enterprise surveys with AI-moderated 1:1 video conversations at scale. - Twilio Q4 voice AI revenue grew 60% as the company closed its biggest enterprise deal ever and repositioned as AI infrastructure. - Regal AI launches Copilot, a self-improving voice agent builder that learns from call outcomes and flags underperformance automatically. - Exotel acquires Dubverse core team to lead [...] The age of "chatting" with AI is officially over. This week, the industry moved from conversation to action with three massive launches. 1. 💸 ChatGPT as your Financial Advisor OpenAI teamed up with Plaid, giving the AI a direct look at your bank and 401k. I tested this with my recent travel budget, and it caught a double-subscription charge I hadn’t noticed in months. It’s like having a CFO in your pocket. 2. 🏪 Claude for the Side Hustle Anthropic just launched a version for small businesses that plugs into QuickBooks and Stripe. Pro-tip: Use the new "Team Memory" feature to store your brand voice guidelines so your marketing drafts actually sound like you from day one. 3. 💻 The Googlebook Rebrand Google is ditching the Chromebook for a Gemini-integrated laptop. This isn't just a browser; [...] automatically. - Exotel acquires Dubverse core team to lead conversation quality analytics and AI, deepening its voice AI stack for Indian enterprises. - Californians sue Sutter and MemorialCare over use of Abridge AI scribe that allegedly recorded doctor-patient visits without clear patient consent. - Five9 expands Fusion ecosystem with AI Agent Connect API, letting enterprises wire voice AI agents into third-party systems and Assembled WFM. - weya AI open-sources Hush, an 8MB speech enhancement model with 1.8M params that isolates the primary speaker in under 1ms per frame, CPU-only. - Shunya Labs launches voice AI platform for dubbing, translation, lip-sync, and low-shot voice cloning for entertainment localization. - Beaver AI launches Magic Whiteboard, a privacy-first meeting

results.title: 100 things we announced at I/O 2026
results.content:
49. The new Antigravity is unabashedly agent-first, focusing on the core agent conversations, agent-produced artifacts and multi-agent orchestration. The Antigravity agent harness, the invisible framework for Gemini to perform real-world tasks, has become much more powerful, with new core primitives such as subagents, hooks and asynchronous task management. Underpinning all of this are the Gemini models, with Gemini 3.5 Flash having been co-optimized with the Antigravity agent harness.

50. Multi-day engineering efforts are collapsing into hours, if not minutes. This is made possible by the new subagent teamwork capability. We’re bringing this to you as an early research preview in Antigravity. [...] 4. Landing in the top-right quadrant of the Artificial Analysis index, 3.5 Flash delivers frontier-level intelligence at exceptional speed — proving you no longer have to trade quality for latency.

5. Gemini 3.5 Flash is ideal for tackling long-horizon agentic tasks. What used to take a developer days or an auditor weeks, 3.5 Flash can now help complete in a fraction of the time, often at less than half the cost of other frontier models. It rapidly plans, builds and iterates to solve real-world problems, whether it’s developing new applications, maintaining codebases or helping to prepare financial documents. Building on the strong multimodal foundation of Gemini 3, 3.5 Flash generates richer, more interactive web UIs and graphics.

results.title: The Top 100 Gen AI Consumer Apps — 6th Edition
results.content:
Finally, AI is increasingly embedded in the tools people already use. Anthropic launched Claude in Excel and Claude in PowerPoint. OpenAI launched ChatGPT for Excel. Google has deepened Gemini’s integration across Workspace — Docs, Sheets, Gmail, and Meet all now have native AI capabilities. Google also launched Personal Intelligence in January 2026, connecting Gemini to Gmail, Google Photos, YouTube, and Search — so the assistant can reference your hotel booking, your purchase history, your photo library, and your watch habits without being told. #### Olivia Moore

   X
   Linkedin [...] Image 7: A chart titled: Sessions per month on Gemini are climbing... but OpenAI still leads.Image 8: A chart titled: ChatGPT and Gemini have unparalleled retention.The next layer of lock-in is the app store. ChatGPT and Claude have both launched connector ecosystems — GPTs and Apps for ChatGPT, MCP integrations and Connectors for Claude — that let users build workflows on top of the assistant. Once a user has configured their AI to talk to their calendar, email, and CRM, switching costs rise dramatically. Developers may concentrate their efforts on the platforms that attract the most users, creating the same kind of flywheel that defined earlier platform wars.

results.title: Top 50 AI Tools for News Making in 2026
results.content:
Claude – Deep research and document summarization. Example: Summarizing lengthy policy reports for news writing.
   ChatGPT – Idea generation, research assistance, and article drafting. Example: Creating multiple headline options and drafting news stories.
   Perplexity AI – Real-time web search with citations. Example: Verifying election results using credible sources.
   Feedly – AI-powered news aggregation. Example: Tracking climate change news from multiple media outlets.
   Artifact – Smart news discovery and summarization. Example: Summarizing long investigative reports.
   SmartNews – Personalized news recommendations. Example: Filtering political news based on user interests.
   Flipboard – Curated digital news magazines. Example: Creating a "Pakistan Politics" news magazine. [...] Factiverse – AI-powered fact-checking. Example: Verifying viral health claims.
   ClaimBuster – Political statement verification. Example: Fact-checking debate statements in real time.
   Full Fact AI – Automated fact verification. Example: Validating COVID-19-related claims.
   Logically AI – Misinformation detection. Example: Detecting election propaganda and false narratives.
   Originality AI – Plagiarism and AI-content detection. Example: Checking whether a news article has been copied.
   NewsGuard – News credibility rating. Example: Assessing the trustworthiness of news websites.
   TrueMedia – Digital media forensic verification. Example: Detecting manipulated images and videos.
   Deepware Scanner – Deepfake detection. Example: Verifying fake politician videos.
